heading: Sale or assignment of loans and underlying security
label: 1485 Sale or assignment of loans and underlying security
notes: References in TextSubsection (i), referred to in subsec. (g)(2), was redesignated as subsection (h) of this section by , , .Pub. L. 109–221, title IV, § 401(b)(3)120 Stat. 342May 12, 2006

L. 109–221, § 401(b)(6), redesignated subsec. (g) as (f). Former subsec. (f) redesignated (e).Pub. L. 109–221, § 401(b)(3)Subsec. (g) to (i). , redesignated subsecs. (a), inserted heading and substituted “Any loan guaranteed or insured” for “Any loan guaranteed”, and added subsecs. (b) to (i).Pub. L. 107–3311988— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section read as follows: “Any loan guaranteed hereunder, including the security given therefor, may be sold or assigned by the lender to any financial institution subject to examination and supervision by an agency of the United States or of any State or the District of Columbia.”Pub. L. 100–442

Findings and Purpose, , , provided that:Pub. L. 107–331, title I, § 102116 Stat. 2835Dec. 13, 2002“(a).—FindingsCongress finds that—“(1)the Indian Financing Act of 1974 ( et seq.) was intended to provide Native American borrowers with access to commercial sources of capital that otherwise would not be available through the guarantee or insurance of loans by the Secretary of the Interior;25 U.S.C. 1451“(2)although the Secretary of the Interior has made loan guarantees and insurance available, use of those guarantees and that insurance by lenders to benefit Native American business borrowers has been limited;“(3)twenty-seven years after the date of enactment of the Indian Financing Act of 1974 ( et seq.) [, which was approved ], the promotion and development of Native American-owned business remains an essential foundation for growth of economic and social stability of Native Americans;25 U.S.C. 1451Pub. L. 93–262Apr. 12, 1974“(4)use by commercial lenders of the available loan insurance and guarantees may be limited by liquidity and other capital market-driven concerns; and“(5)it is in the best interest of the insured and guaranteed loan program of the Department of the Interior—“(A)to encourage the orderly development and expansion of a secondary market for loans guaranteed or insured by the Secretary of the Interior; and“(B)to expand the number of lenders originating loans under the Indian Financing Act of 1974 ( et seq.).25 U.S.C. 1451“(b).—PurposeThe purpose of this Act [see Short Title of 2002 Amendment note set out under ] is to reform and clarify the Indian Financing Act of 1974 ( et seq.) in order to—section 1451 of this title25 U.S.C. 1451“(1)stimulate the use by lenders of secondary market investors for loans guaranteed or insured under a program administered by the Secretary of the Interior;“(2)preserve the authority of the Secretary to administer the program and regulate lenders;“(3)clarify that a good faith investor in loans insured or guaranteed by the Secretary will receive appropriate payments;“(4)provide for the appointment by the Secretary of a qualified fiscal transfer agent to establish and administer a system for the orderly transfer of those loans; and“(5)(A)authorize the Secretary to promulgate regulations to encourage and expand a secondary market program for loans guaranteed or insured by the Secretary; and“(B)allow the pooling of those loans as the secondary market develops.”

children.text: may be transferred by the lender by sale or assignment to any person; and
may be retransferred by the transferee.
the transfer shall be consistent with such regulations as the Secretary shall promulgate under subsection (h); and
the transferee shall give notice of the transfer to the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The full faith and credit of the United States is pledged to the payment of all loan guarantees and loan insurance made under this subchapter after .December 13, 2002

children.heading: Validity
children.label: 2 Validity
children.text: Except as provided in regulations in effect on the date on which a loan is made, the validity of a guarantee or insurance of a loan under this subchapter shall be incontestable.

children.heading: Damages
children.label: d Damages
children.text: Notwithstanding , the Secretary may recover from a lender of a loan under this subchapter any damages suffered by the Secretary as a result of a material breach of the obligations of the lender with respect to a guarantee or insurance by the Secretary of the loan.section 3302 of title 31

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may collect a fee for any loan or guaranteed or insured portion of a loan that is transferred in accordance with this section.

children.heading: Compensation of fiscal transfer agent
children.label: 2 Compensation of fiscal transfer agent
children.text: A fiscal transfer agent designated under subsection (f) may be compensated through any of the fees assessed under this section and any interest earned on any funds or fees collected by the fiscal transfer agent while the funds or fees are in the control of the fiscal transfer agent and before the time at which the fiscal transfer agent is contractually required to transfer such funds to the Secretary or to transferees or other holders.
provide for a central registration of all guaranteed or insured loans transferred under this section; and

children.label: A
children.text: to act as the designee of the Secretary under this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: to carry out on behalf of the Secretary the central registration and fiscal transfer agent functions under this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Nothing in this subchapter prohibits the pooling of whole loans or interests in loans transferred under this section.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 2 Regulations
children.text: In promulgating regulations under subsection (i), the Secretary may include such regulations to effect orderly and efficient pooling procedures as the Secretary determines to be necessary.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: h Regulations
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary shall develop such procedures and promulgate such regulations as are necessary to facilitate, administer, and promote transfers of loans and guaranteed and insured portions of loans under this section.December 13, 2002
